2606.06380.title: Emergent Language as an Approach to Conscious AI
2606.06380.paper_summary: The question of whether artificial systems can be conscious remains open, in part because existing approaches either evaluate systems against theory-derived checklists (discriminative) or engineer consciousness-inspired modules directly (architectural); both leave open whether observed structures are artifacts of human language priors. We propose a generative methodology: emergent language (EL) in multi-agent reinforcement learning, where agents start from minimal (no language, no concept of self, minimal exposure to human text) and develop communication under task pressure alone, ensuring causal attributability to task demands rather than inherited human language priors. We position our methodology by discussing how EL serves as a generative tool for studying consciousness-relevant structure, including the role of environment complexity and the interpretation of emergent communication. As a proof of concept, we instantiate this methodology in a minimal environment and show that agents develop self-referential communication, including an echo-mismatch detection circuit that is not predicted by task structure or architecture alone but emerges from a specific environmental affordance.
2606.06380.paper_summary_zh: 人工系統是否能夠具備意識的問題仍然懸而未決，部分原因在於現有的方法要麼是根據理論衍生的檢查清單來評估系統（區分性），要麼是直接工程化靈感來自意識的模塊（架構性）；這兩者都未解決觀察到的結構是否是人類語言先驗的產物。我們提出了一種生成性方法論：在多智能體強化學習中的新興語言（EL），其中智能體從最小的狀態（無語言、無自我概念、對人類文本的最小接觸）開始，並在任務壓力下發展溝通，確保因果歸因於任務需求，而非繼承的人類語言先驗。我們通過討論EL如何作為研究與意識相關結構的生成工具來定位我們的方法論，包括環境複雜性和新興溝通的解釋的角色。作為概念驗證，我們在一個最小環境中實現這一方法論，並展示智能體發展出自我參照的溝通，包括一個回聲不匹配檢測電路，該電路並不是僅由任務結構或架構預測的，而是從特定的環境可供性中產生的。
2606.06380.author: Zengqing Wu et.al.
